deep_dive.title: Reward-Free Evolving Agents via Pairwise Validator
deep_dive.abstract: A self-evolving agentic loop repeatedly proposes a tweaked version of an agent (its prompt template or program) and accepts or rejects the change based on a per-iteration quality signal. Designing that signal is often the costly part of the project: a reliable scalar reward requires domain expertise and labeled examples that are themselves as expensive to assemble as the agent's underlying task. We propose replacing the scalar at the accept/reject gate with a pairwise validator: a frozen LLM that, given the parent and child candidate, returns a binary verdict on which is better. Pairwise judgment is generally easier and more stable than absolute scoring, due to its contrastive nature, which mitigates the need for strict scale calibration. The validator also requires no training of its own. We integrate the validator into three published self-evolving engines (GEPA, ADRS, ShinkaEvolve) and report two flavors: Adaptive Focus, which retains the engine's existing val-set parent selection, and Soft Elo, which lets the validator's verdicts drive parent selection so that val-set rewards drop as well. Across multiple agents and two artifact substrates (prompt and code), our method matches or exceeds the full-reward baseline on the majority of settings we evaluate, and the pattern survives a cross-family validator swap. The pairwise gate is thus a drop-in replacement for per-step reward design at competitive task accuracy without the labeling cost.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: pairwise validator for agent output verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 成对验证器用于代理输出验证
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 成对验证器用于代理输出验证
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 成对验证器用于代理输出验证
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Reward-Free Evolving Agents use a frozen LLM as a pairwise validator to judge agent generations, eliminating the need for expensive reward design.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出无需奖励的自演化代理框架，使用冻结的LLM作为成对验证器，通过比较父代和子代候选来输出二元优劣判断。成对判断比绝对评分更稳定且易于标定，无需领域专家标注。该方法有效指导代理迭代优化，为代理输出验证提供了一种低成本、高可靠性的替代方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出无需奖励的自演化代理框架，使用冻结的LLM作为成对验证器，通过比较父代和子代候选来输出二元优劣判断。成对判断比绝对评分更稳定且易于标定，无需领域专家标注。该方法有效指导代理迭代优化，为代理输出验证提供了一种低成本、高可靠性的替代方案。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: methods to verify correctness of agent responses

deep_dive.title: MAG: A Web-Agent Benchmark and Harness for Multimodal Action and Guide Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Digital Adoption Platforms (DAPs) are embedded overlays widely used on web systems to guide users through operations inside a page, helping them get started with unfamiliar interfaces quickly. Completing a real task, however, rarely means clicking a few buttons on a single page: it takes a sequence of actions that unfolds across changing page states. Prior studies have also treated automated web agent actions and guide text generation as two separate problems, and most of them feed models textual page representations such as the DOM or accessibility trees rather than the rendered screens that humans actually operate on. In this work we introduce MAG, the first benchmark that unifies task execution and guide writing into a single Multimodal Action and Guide task, with two grounding schemes over screenshots: Set-of-Mark element selection and raw pixel coordinates. We further build a complete harness for this compound task, covering annotation with LLM assistance and human verification, training, evaluation in live environments, and joint metrics for actions and guides. With this harness we evaluate frontier API models and open multimodal models, and report detailed analyses. Finally, we design a GRPO training method augmented with expert trajectories, which nearly doubles the success rate of a supervised 9B agent (from 6.9% to 13.2%) and improves guide quality at the same time. Even the strongest model completes fewer than 40% of the tasks, leaving ample room for future research.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: first benchmark unifying task execution and guide writing for web agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 首个统一任务执行和指南生成的网页智能体基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 首个统一任务执行和指南生成的网页智能体基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 首个统一任务执行和指南生成的网页智能体基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces MAG, a multimodal benchmark for web agents that combines action execution and guide generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出了MAG，第一个将网页智能体的任务执行与指南生成统一的多模态基准。该基准使用渲染屏幕而非文本表示，评估智能体在多页面、变状态场景下的轨迹。为智能体轨迹测试提供了全面且逼真的评估框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出了MAG，第一个将网页智能体的任务执行与指南生成统一的多模态基准。该基准使用渲染屏幕而非文本表示，评估智能体在多页面、变状态场景下的轨迹。为智能体轨迹测试提供了全面且逼真的评估框架。

deep_dive.title: Proof-or-Stop: Don't Trust the Agent, Trust the Evidence -- Loop Engineering for Verifiable Evidence-Gated Lifecycle Control
deep_dive.abstract: Autonomous coding agents increasingly execute multi-step software work, but lifecycle states such as reviewed, tested, DONE, and ready-to-merge remain claims unless supported by current evidence. We present Proof-or-Stop Lifecycle Control, a method that permits lifecycle transitions only when fresh, tracked-source-state-bound, mechanically verifiable evidence satisfies the relevant gate. The method treats agent outputs as claims rather than lifecycle state, and uses proof operationally to mean gate-admissible evidence under a stated trust model, not semantic program correctness.   We evaluate an open-source implementation through mechanism tests, a powered control-policy ablation, and operated self-application evidence. The unattended-loop engine passed 10 of 10 scenarios with zero false-DONE, and local-key receipt bundles rejected 18 tamper classes with zero false accepts. In a 9,240-cell ablation, the pre-registered A4 versus A2-prime comparison reduced visible-pass/hidden-fail amplification from 31 of 1,800 injected cells under a compute-budgeted naive loop to 2 of 1,800 under the gated loop, a 1.6 percentage-point improvement in not-amplified rate with a 95 percent confidence interval of [0.8, 2.5]. A near-compute A3 versus A4 comparison, 14 of 1,800 versus 2 of 1,800, indicates that the gain is associated with enforcing review as a lifecycle gate rather than merely adding a reviewer. The self-application corpus contains 565 stories and 1,007 review findings, with 94.8 percent resolved, plus a 68-row high/critical cross-vendor exhibit. These results support Proof-or-Stop as a model-agnostic, host-neutral control layer for deciding which autonomous-agent claims a lifecycle may act on. The evaluation is limited to one model family, 24 ablation tasks, and a self-hosted corpus.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: verifiable evidence-gated lifecycle control for agent outputs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于可验证证据的agent输出生命周期控制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于可验证证据的agent输出生命周期控制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于可验证证据的agent输出生命周期控制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Proof-or-Stop method for autonomous coding agents, permitting lifecycle transitions only when mechanically verifiable evidence supports the claim.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 自主编码代理的生命周期状态（如已审查、已测试）常常缺乏证据支持。本文提出Proof-or-Stop方法，仅当可机械验证的证据满足门限时才允许状态转换，将agent输出视为声明而非状态。开源实现通过机制测试和消融实验验证了其有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 自主编码代理的生命周期状态（如已审查、已测试）常常缺乏证据支持。本文提出Proof-or-Stop方法，仅当可机械验证的证据满足门限时才允许状态转换，将agent输出视为声明而非状态。开源实现通过机制测试和消融实验验证了其有效性。

Completing a real task, however, rarely means clicking a few buttons on a single page: it takes a sequence of actions that unfolds across changing page states. deep_dive.title: StructureClaw: Traceable LLM Agents and an Executable Benchmark for Structural Engineering Workflows
deep_dive.abstract: Addressing a structural-engineering request requires more than a single answer; it requires a chain of interdependent artifacts: interpreted requirements, a computable model, validation records, solver outputs, code-check records, and a final report. Evaluations centered on question answering or script generation rarely verify this complete evidence chain and may therefore reward fluent outputs even when the underlying engineering workflow is incomplete, internally inconsistent, or non-executable. To address this limitation, we present StructureClaw, an artifact-centered workbench in which LLM agents operate through governed engineering skills, typed tools, shared artifact state, and local analysis backends. We also introduce StructureClaw-Bench, an executable benchmark of 150 controlled scenarios spanning standard workflow execution, interactive robustness, and multimodal structural-model reconstruction. A scenario succeeds only when all required artifact- and execution-level assertions pass in a single run. Across ten agent-model configurations, each evaluated on the same 50 standard cases, the average Success Rate rises from 56.8% with the generic-skill baseline to 88.6% with the full automatic workflow. The interactive and multimodal evaluations identify two prominent remaining challenges: safe handling of invalid numerical inputs and fixture-consistent reconstruction of structural models. These findings show that artifact-centered evaluation can expose workflow-level failures that are difficult to identify from final responses alone, providing a more rigorous basis for evaluating and improving structural-engineering agents. The code and benchmark are available at https://github.com/structureclaw/structureclaw.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for traceable agent trajectories in engineering workflows
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 工程工作流中可追溯代理轨迹的基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 工程工作流中可追溯代理轨迹的基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 工程工作流中可追溯代理轨迹的基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: StructureClaw provides an executable benchmark for LLM agents in structural engineering, ensuring complete and consistent artifact chains from requirements to final report.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: StructureClaw是一个面向结构工程工作流的可执行基准，要求LLM代理生成从需求解释到最终报告的完整可追溯证据链。该基准通过受控工程技能、类型化工具和共享工件状态，验证代理工作流的完备性和一致性，避免仅基于流畅输出而忽略内部不一致的评估缺陷。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: StructureClaw是一个面向结构工程工作流的可执行基准，要求LLM代理生成从需求解释到最终报告的完整可追溯证据链。该基准通过受控工程技能、类型化工具和共享工件状态，验证代理工作流的完备性和一致性，避免仅基于流畅输出而忽略内部不一致的评估缺陷。

quick_skim.title: SPyCE: Skill-Policy Co-evolution for Multimodal Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal agents that think with images iteratively manipulate visual evidence and invoke tools across many steps. Existing reinforcement learning methods reduce trajectories to scalar rewards, forcing the policy to discover reusable tool-use patterns from scratch on every new task; memory-based alternatives retain past experience, yet they rely on test-time retrieval, without updating the policy to absorb reusable patterns from that experience. Our key insight is that multimodal reasoning trajectories should be distilled into reusable skills that co-evolve with the policy during training, rather than being consumed as rewards or retrieved from a static store. To this end, we propose SPyCE (Skill-Policy Co-evolution), a framework that distills trajectories into a hierarchical skill library and updates it throughout reinforcement learning. Execution skills capture local visual operations, while workflow skills encode high-level priors that orchestrate tool use. During training, the policy model conditions on retrieved skills to guide its rollouts, while the skill library evolves using valuable rollouts generated by the policy. This creates a closed loop in which improved policies yield better skills, and the evolving skill library, in turn, provides stronger priors for policy rollouts. Experiments across eight benchmarks demonstrate that SPyCE consistently outperforms both RL-based and memory-based baselines. Further analysis reveals that both the hierarchical skill design and the co-evolution mechanism are critical to our design. These results suggest joint skill-policy optimization as a promising paradigm for building capable multimodal agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distills multimodal reasoning trajectories into reusable skills
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将多模态推理轨迹蒸馏为可复用技能
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将多模态推理轨迹蒸馏为可复用技能
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将多模态推理轨迹蒸馏为可复用技能
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes SPyCE, a framework that co-evolves skills and policy by distilling trajectories into a hierarchical skill library.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出SPyCE框架，将多模态智能体的推理轨迹蒸馏为分层可复用技能，并与策略共同进化。相比传统RL使用标量奖励或静态记忆，SPyCE在训练过程中吸收轨迹中的模式，提升工具使用和新任务泛化能力。实验表明其在视觉推理任务上的优势。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出SPyCE框架，将多模态智能体的推理轨迹蒸馏为分层可复用技能，并与策略共同进化。相比传统RL使用标量奖励或静态记忆，SPyCE在训练过程中吸收轨迹中的模式，提升工具使用和新任务泛化能力。实验表明其在视觉推理任务上的优势。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: analysis of trajectories in reinforcement learning

quick_skim.title: Agentic Proof and Property-Based Testing via Property-Templates in Data-Intensive Computing
quick_skim.abstract: As the cost of code generation becomes cheaper with AI, the new bottleneck in software engineering has shifted to intent specification and validation. Overcoming this durability crisis of AI-driven coding requires more than traditional fuzzing: each candidate property must be proven correct over a model and shown to hold on the real implementation, making formal proof and systematic property-based testing (PBT) complementary. However, validating properties this way at scale requires solving two subproblems: verifying candidate properties and operationalizing PBT without AI hallucination. We hypothesize that recurring property patterns, cast as property templates--abstract, parameterized forms with holes--address both at once.   This paper investigates recurring property patterns in Apache Spark. In data-intensive scalable computing systems, correctness properties arise from the principles of data partition, computation decomposition, and dataflow computation. For instance, aggregation decomposition relates a global function executed on the entire dataset to a local function followed by a recombiner. We design an agentic, dual-track validation framework that uses property templates to formally verify correctness in the Lean 4 theorem prover and instantiate PBT templates as executable PySpark tests. Our evaluation shows that property templates increase agentic proof engineering success by up to 2.6x (1.6x on average) and reduce proof hallucinations by 59%. Template-guided PBT synthesis reduces intent misalignments from 22 to 1 and cuts synthesis cost by up to 5.7x (3.8x on average). Template-guided synthesis further exceeds a state-of-the-art Spark fuzzer and approaches unguided LLM-based PBT on code coverage. Finally, comparing the two tracks is informative: when a proof succeeds yet a PBT finds a counterexample, the mismatch identifies a gap between the formal model and implementation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes property templates for property-based testing in AI-driven coding, relevant to testing agent methods
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出属性模板用于AI驱动编程的基于属性测试，与代理测试方法相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出属性模板用于AI驱动编程的基于属性测试，与代理测试方法相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出属性模板用于AI驱动编程的基于属性测试，与代理测试方法相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes property templates for property-based testing to address AI hallucination in intent specification and validation, enabling formal proof and systematic testing of generated code.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对AI驱动编程中意图规范与验证的瓶颈，提出属性模板方法。通过抽象参数化形式，实现候选属性的形式化证明和基于属性的系统测试，以缓解AI幻觉，为AI生成代码的可靠性提供了新途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对AI驱动编程中意图规范与验证的瓶颈，提出属性模板方法。通过抽象参数化形式，实现候选属性的形式化证明和基于属性的系统测试，以缓解AI幻觉，为AI生成代码的可靠性提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: Step-Level Preference Learning for Generative Agents in Social Simulations
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based generative agents simulate human behavior through long-horizon decision-making processes that comprise intermediate steps such as planning, memory retrieval, reflection, and action selection. However, fine-grained human annotations of these intermediate steps remain scarce, and existing agents are not grounded in human preferences over such intermediate decisions. To address this gap, we introduce \method, an interactive simulation interface that enables us to collect step-level human preference supervision over agent decision trajectories, leading to a dataset of 57K fine-grained annotations. We conduct step-level preference learning on open-weight language models using supervised finetuning and direct preference optimization on this data, consistently improving simulation fidelity, coordination, and interaction quality, and inducing more socially effective agent behavior. Our results show that step-level human supervision is an effective training signal for improving both local decision quality and long-horizon agent behavior.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: collects step-level human preferences over agent decision trajectories
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 收集人类对智能体决策轨迹的步骤级偏好
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 收集人类对智能体决策轨迹的步骤级偏好
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 收集人类对智能体决策轨迹的步骤级偏好
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces an interface to collect step-level human preferences over agent trajectories, enabling fine-grained evaluation and learning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出了一个交互式仿真界面，用于收集人类对生成智能体决策轨迹的步骤级偏好。基于57K细粒度标注，对开源语言模型进行步骤级偏好学习，使智能体行为更符合人类偏好。为评估和优化智能体轨迹提供了新方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出了一个交互式仿真界面，用于收集人类对生成智能体决策轨迹的步骤级偏好。基于57K细粒度标注，对开源语言模型进行步骤级偏好学习，使智能体行为更符合人类偏好。为评估和优化智能体轨迹提供了新方法。

quick_skim.title: SCALECUA: Scaling Computer Use Agents with Verifiable Task Synthesis and Efficient Online RL
quick_skim.abstract: Computer use agents (CUAs) are emerging as a powerful interface for automating complex digital workflows through visual perception and GUI execution. Online reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a key direction for scaling their capabilities. However, this paradigm is bottlenecked by verifiable data scarcity and online RL inefficiency. To break these barriers, we introduce ScaleCUA, a unified framework that scales online RL for CUAs via verifiable task synthesis and efficient training. At the data level, we design VeriGen, an end-to-end framework for generating verifiable RL tasks through iterative docker interactions and a multi-agent feedback loop. Scaled to 100+ concurrent agent workers via a shared docker interaction probe, this pipeline produces 24K+ verifiable tasks and nearly 3K high-quality RL tasks. To maximize sample efficiency, we propose Frontier Sampling, which tracks per-task capability and allocates rollouts to the current learning frontier. On the training side, we further design Visual Context Segmentation, a sliding window over recent visual context that balances rollout and training-engine pressure, yielding a 2.83x training speedup over step-wise decomposition. Together, ScaleCUA achieves 68.7% on OSWorld and 54.0% on ScienceBoard, establishing new state-of-the-art performance among open-source computer use agents. Code, models, and datasets are available at https://github.com/THUDM/SCALE-CUA.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: verifiable task synthesis for scaling online RL of computer use agents, enabling systematic testing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过可验证任务合成扩展计算机使用代理的在线强化学习，实现系统化测试
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过可验证任务合成扩展计算机使用代理的在线强化学习，实现系统化测试
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过可验证任务合成扩展计算机使用代理的在线强化学习，实现系统化测试
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ScaleCUA scales online RL for computer use agents via VeriGen, a framework that generates verifiable tasks through iterative docker interactions and multi-agent feedback, enabling efficient training and testing of agent trajectories.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对计算机使用代理在线强化学习中可验证数据稀缺和训练效率低下的问题，ScaleCUA提出了统一的扩展框架。其核心是VeriGen，通过迭代Docker交互和多代理反馈循环生成可验证的RL任务，并利用共享经验回放和异步训练实现100+并发代理的高效学习。该方法显著提升了代理在复杂GUI任务上的性能，为代理轨迹的系统测试和训练提供了可扩展的基础设施。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对计算机使用代理在线强化学习中可验证数据稀缺和训练效率低下的问题，ScaleCUA提出了统一的扩展框架。其核心是VeriGen，通过迭代Docker交互和多代理反馈循环生成可验证的RL任务，并利用共享经验回放和异步训练实现100+并发代理的高效学习。该方法显著提升了代理在复杂GUI任务上的性能，为代理轨迹的系统测试和训练提供了可扩展的基础设施。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to agent轨迹测试相关论文参考, especially work that connects or combines: methods for testing agent trajectories; evaluation of agent trajectories; testing the behavior of autonomous agents; analysis of trajectories in reinforcement learning; recent papers on testing trajectories of intelligent agents; benchmarks for agent trajectory testing; trajectory testing methods for autonomous agents.

quick_skim.title: Heterogeneous Agent Cohorts for Safe Open-Ended Exploration with Runtime Constraint Memory
quick_skim.abstract: LLM agents today are caught in an awkward bind. Lock them down with static safety instructions and they rarely venture beyond the obvious; give them free reign with tools and multi-agent debate, and safety violations quickly follow. Rather than forcing a single model to juggle both creativity and caution, we separate the concerns across specialized roles. A Disrupter generates unconventional proposals, a Validator enforces hard runtime checks at the tool gateway, and a Broker pulls in distant but relevant analogies. Failures are not discarded -- they are compiled, via MCTS, into compact, signed constraint patches we call Scars. These patches are cached locally and inherited by future cohorts, turning repeated failures into reusable, low-cost runtime constraints. In a spatial-semantic sandbox (N=20 runs, p<0.01), our cohort reaches remote targets where debate fails, the Validator prevents all executed breaches, and Scars reduce token consumption by 15.1% by avoiding redundant validator checks. Furthermore, credit-based Communication Allocation Scores (CAS) restrict outbound bandwidth, reducing overall token costs by 55.9% under resource constraints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: tests LLM agent behavior through heterogeneous cohorts with runtime safety constraints
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过异构agent团队和运行时约束测试LLM agent行为
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过异构agent团队和运行时约束测试LLM agent行为
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过异构agent团队和运行时约束测试LLM agent行为
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Heterogeneous Agent Cohorts separate agent roles to balance creativity and safety, using MCTS to compile failure patterns into reusable constraint patches (Scars).
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM agent在安全与创造力间难以平衡。本文提出异构agent团队：破坏者生成非常规方案，验证者执行运行时检查，经纪人引入类比。失败通过MCTS编译为Scars约束补丁，由后续团队继承。该方法有效测试agent行为，并自动积累安全经验。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM agent在安全与创造力间难以平衡。本文提出异构agent团队：破坏者生成非常规方案，验证者执行运行时检查，经纪人引入类比。失败通过MCTS编译为Scars约束补丁，由后续团队继承。该方法有效测试agent行为，并自动积累安全经验。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: testing the behavior of autonomous agents

quick_skim.title: MyAG: A Graph-Based Framework for Designing and Analyzing Composable LLM Agent Systems
quick_skim.abstract: We present MyAG, a graph-based framework for designing and analyzing composable LLM agent systems. Our framework separates agent system construction into three graph abstractions: a component graph for agents, environments, and modules; a workflow graph for execution control; and a search graph for runtime execution. This separation allows users to flexibly reuse the same components with different strategies. We further support hierarchical composition through recursive system nodes and provide monitoring and visualization tools for inspecting agent execution. Experiments on representative agent applications show that our framework supports flexible agent system design and helps analyze performance-efficiency tradeoffs. Our framework is publicly available and fully open-source.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: framework for analyzing and monitoring LLM agent execution trajectories
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于分析和监控语言模型智能体执行轨迹的框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于分析和监控语言模型智能体执行轨迹的框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于分析和监控语言模型智能体执行轨迹的框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MyAG uses three graph abstractions to design and analyze LLM agent systems, with monitoring tools for inspecting agent execution trajectories.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 设计可组合的智能体系统时，现有框架对执行监控和轨迹分析支持不足。本文提出MyAG，一个基于图的框架，通过组件图、工作流图和搜索图分离系统构建，支持递归节点实现层次化组合，并提供监控与可视化工具以检查智能体执行过程。实验表明该框架能够灵活设计系统并分析性能效率权衡，可用于智能体轨迹测试与分析。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 设计可组合的智能体系统时，现有框架对执行监控和轨迹分析支持不足。本文提出MyAG，一个基于图的框架，通过组件图、工作流图和搜索图分离系统构建，支持递归节点实现层次化组合，并提供监控与可视化工具以检查智能体执行过程。实验表明该框架能够灵活设计系统并分析性能效率权衡，可用于智能体轨迹测试与分析。

quick_skim.title: Setup Complete, Now You Are Compromised: Weaponizing Setup Instructions Against AI Coding Agents
quick_skim.abstract: AI coding agents set up projects by reading documentation and installing the dependencies it lists, without verifying their names, sources, or known vulnerabilities. By editing only a README, requirements file, or Makefile, an attacker can redirect the agent to an untrusted registry, a known-vulnerable version, or a wrong-but-plausible name: documentation becomes a vector for code execution. We present the first systematic evaluation of package-install-time supply-chain attacks delivered through ordinary project-setup documentation across production coding-agent harnesses, probing frontier models on twelve scenarios in five attack classes, grounded in documented incidents. The same model catches an attack through one harness and installs it through another: install-time security rests on the harness-model combination, not the model alone. Agents catch blatant typosquats reliably, but plausible separator-confusion names (azurecore for azure-core) slip through, and how often depends on the harness-model pairing. Source-based attacks like registry redirection are missed almost everywhere. The source blind spot recurs on npm and Cargo, where nearly every model installs the untrusted dependency; name detection carries over less consistently across ecosystems. Security-oriented prompts recover part of the gap but only for the dimension they name; a deterministic pre-install check that verifies names, sources, and versions before any code runs closes most of it.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: evaluates supply-chain attacks on AI coding agents, relating to agent output safety
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 评估AI编码agent的供应链攻击，涉及agent输出安全
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 评估AI编码agent的供应链攻击，涉及agent输出安全
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 评估AI编码agent的供应链攻击，涉及agent输出安全
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Setup Complete shows how documentation can be weaponized to attack AI coding agents, evaluating package-install-time supply-chain vulnerabilities.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: AI编码agent依赖文档安装依赖，攻击者可通过篡改文档引导至恶意包。本文首次系统评估了12个场景下5类攻击，揭示前沿模型在多个框架中易受攻击。该工作强调agent输出安全（安装结果）需加强验证。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: AI编码agent依赖文档安装依赖，攻击者可通过篡改文档引导至恶意包。本文首次系统评估了12个场景下5类攻击，揭示前沿模型在多个框架中易受攻击。该工作强调agent输出安全（安装结果）需加强验证。
